violations.text: Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Homemade green dressing was placed in the cold drawer unit and stacked in single service plastic containers in a container greater than four inches deep. Per PIC, items were made under 3 hours prior to inspection. Food moved to the walk-in freezer to rapidly cool. Cooling methods reviewed with PIC at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2024

Thermometers are lacking in the prep line refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2024

Various foods were observed held at 44-48 degrees Fahrenheit condiments cold draw unit and sandwich prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, items made in house were placed in the condiments cold draw unit over four hours prior to inspection. PIC voluntarily disposed of items. Per PIC, items in the sandwich prep unit were placed in there two hours prior to inspection. Items moved to walk in cooler to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2024

Thermometers are lacking in the prep line refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2023

The condiments cold drawer and the sandwich prep unit was observed to be in poor repair (not maintaining an ambient temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below). Unit to not be used until they can be serviced/replaced/repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine, fan covers in walk in, knife holder, vent in drive thru, have an accumulation of dust, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2024
The hand-washing sink in the back kitchen and drive thru was lacking paper towels. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (soup ladle and vegetable slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2023

Thermometers are lacking in the several refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the soups wells, drive thru soda machine, front counter beverage cooler, milk coolers, prep units, dessert racks. and shelves throughout kitchen and drive thru area have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 11/03/2023
The ceilings in walk in cooler have an accumulation of dust. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the back outside area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2023
